project.objective: Identify the father of Patrick Flynn, born ca. 1845 in Pennsylvania, died 1908 in Schuylkill County, PA

questions.question: Who was the father of Patrick Flynn (b. ~1845, PA, d. 1908)?
questions.rationale: Identifying Patrick's father is the primary research objective. The mother remains unidentified and would be a separate follow-on question.

questions.question: Where was Patrick Flynn in the 1850 census?
questions.rationale: The 1850 census is the earliest enumeration where Patrick would appear by name (age ~5). Locating him in a household identifies candidate parents.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Searched 1850 census for Schuylkill County on FamilySearch (indexed and browse), Ancestry (indexed), and MyHeritage (indexed). All three returned the same household. No other Patrick Flynn of matching age found in the county.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes — Patrick Flynn located in a specific 1850 household, answering the question.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: Three major repositories searched (FamilySearch, Ancestry, MyHeritage). No additional known indexes of the 1850 Schuylkill County census exist.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: FamilySearch provides the original census image; Ancestry index is derivative but confirmed consistent.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: FamilySearch (original) and Ancestry (derivative) both return the same household. Two access paths, one underlying original.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Yes — FamilySearch provides original census image with indeterminate-quality information.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: No conflicts on the 1850 census placement question.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low — all three repositories agree, and no competing Patrick Flynn of matching age exists in the county.

items.rationale: 1850 census fully indexed on FamilySearch. Free access, start here.
Ancestry has independent indexing; cross-check for transcription errors.
Third independent index for coverage confirmation.
Confirm Patrick still in Thomas Flynn household in 1860. Strengthens parent-child identification.

Death certificate may name parents directly. However, Schuylkill County death records for the pre-1906 county filing period were destroyed in a 1920 courthouse fire, and the Pennsylvania State Archives does not hold duplicate copies for this county and period. No online or mail-order access path exists.
Thomas Flynn probate/will may name Patrick as son. Will found — names Patrick Flynn as eldest son and heir.

Later censuses (1870, 1880, 1900) checked for corroboration. 1880 census introduces the 'Relationship to head of household' column but Patrick (age ~35) was enumerated as head of his own household — no parentage evidence. 1870 census found Patrick still in Thomas Flynn's household (age ~25). No contradictory evidence from any later census.

Catholic baptism record could provide primary direct evidence of parentage, but pre-1850 Irish Catholic parish records for Schuylkill County are fragmentary and largely unavailable on FamilySearch. Three converging sources make the father identification defensible without this record type.

Thomas Flynn's naturalization papers may list family members, but naturalization records rarely name children. Three independent sources already confirm parentage. Low value-add for this specific question.

log.notes: Found Patrick Flynn age 5 in household of Thomas Flynn, dwelling 84, Schuylkill Co. Three other Flynn results examined — ages and locations don't match.
Ancestry index confirms same household. Transcription matches FamilySearch except Ancestry reads dwelling as 84, FamilySearch as 84 — consistent.
MyHeritage returned no results for Patrick Flynn in 1850 Schuylkill County. Site may not have this collection indexed. Searched broader Pennsylvania — still no match.
Patrick Flynn age 15 in household of Thomas Flynn, Schuylkill Co. Consistent with 1850 placement.

Will of Thomas Flynn found in Schuylkill County Register of Wills, filed 1882. Names 'my eldest son Patrick Flynn' as principal heir. Also names two other children (Mary and John). Provides direct evidence of the parent-child relationship.

1870 census: Patrick Flynn age 25 in household of Thomas Flynn, Schuylkill Co. — corroborates 1850/1860 placement. 1880 census: Patrick Flynn age 35, head of own household, wife Margaret, no parentage information (relationship column says 'Self'). 1900 census: Patrick Flynn age 55, same household. Later censuses corroborate identity but add no new parentage evidence beyond 1870.

sources.citation: 1850 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, dwelling 84, family 91, Thomas Flynn household; NARA microfilm publication M432, roll 810; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 1 May 2026.
sources.notes: Image quality good. Enumerator handwriting clear.

sources.citation: 1850 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, dwelling 84, Thomas Flynn household; digital image, Ancestry.com, accessed 1 May 2026.

citation_detail.what: 1850 U.S. Federal Census, population schedule (Ancestry index)

sources.notes: Ancestry's index of the same original census. Transcription consistent with FamilySearch.

sources.citation: 1860 U.S. Census, Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania, population schedule, dwelling 112, family 119, Thomas Flynn household; NARA microfilm publication M653, roll 1141; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 2 May 2026.

Will of Thomas Flynn, filed 1882, Schuylkill County Register of Wills, Will Book G, p. 214; Schuylkill County Courthouse, Pottsville, Pennsylvania; digital image, FamilySearch.org, accessed 4 May 2026.
sources.notes: Thomas Flynn's will names 'my eldest son Patrick Flynn' as principal heir. Primary information — the testator is directly stating his own children.

assertions.informant: Unknown informant (most likely a household member, possibly a neighbor) reporting to census enumerator
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Census enumerator is the recorder, not the informant. The person who provided the name is unknown — most likely a household member, possibly a neighbor. Proximity is 'unknown' rather than 'household_member' because for names specifically, the enumerator may have heard the name from a neighbor or made an assumption — the name fact doesn't necessarily require active reporting the way age/birthplace does.

assertions.informant: Unknown — most likely a household member (such as Thomas Flynn or his wife), but possibly a neighbor
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Age and birthplace require active reporting by an informant — someone had to tell the enumerator these facts. That informant was most likely a household member, but enumerators also took information from neighbors when residents were unavailable, and the census does not identify who answered. Proximity is 'unknown' because the informant cannot be established from the record.

assertions.informant: Census enumerator (witness to residence by visiting the dwelling)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: For residence facts specifically, the enumerator is a direct witness — they physically visited the dwelling and recorded who lived there. This distinguishes residence from other census facts where an unidentified informant (most likely a household member, possibly a neighbor) supplied the information.

assertions.value: Listed in household of Thomas Flynn (head), position consistent with child
assertions.informant: Inferred from household structure — no explicit informant for relationships in 1850 census
assertions.informant_bias_notes: 1850 census does not state relationships; this assertion is inferred from household position and shared surname, not directly reported by any informant. The relationship is indirect evidence constructed by the researcher, not information provided by a census informant.

assertions.informant: Unknown informant (likely Thomas Flynn himself) reporting to census enumerator
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Census enumerator is the recorder, not the informant. As head of household, Thomas likely provided his own name, but the 1850 census does not identify the informant.

assertions.informant: Unknown informant (most likely a household member, possibly a neighbor) reporting to census enumerator
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Census enumerator is the recorder, not the informant. Proximity is 'unknown' for names (same reasoning as a_001 — name facts don't necessarily require active reporting).

assertions.informant: Unknown — most likely a household member (such as Thomas Flynn or his wife), but possibly a neighbor
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Age and birthplace require active reporting by an informant — most likely a household member, possibly a neighbor (same reasoning as a_002).

assertions.value: Listed in household of Thomas Flynn (head), position and age consistent with son
assertions.informant: Inferred from household structure — no explicit informant for relationships in 1860 census
assertions.informant_bias_notes: 1860 census does not state relationships; like the 1850 census, this assertion is inferred from household position, age, and shared surname. The relationship column was not introduced until the 1880 census.

assertions.value: Son: Patrick Flynn (named as 'my eldest son Patrick Flynn')
assertions.informant: Thomas Flynn (testator — directly naming his own children in his will)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Primary information — the testator is the father himself, directly stating the parent-child relationship in a legal document. No intermediary reporting.

person_evidence.rationale: Name matches (Patrick Flynn), age consistent with ~1845 birth, located in Schuylkill County where subject is known to have lived. Only Patrick Flynn of this age in the county in 1850.
Same record as pe_001, same person. Household position and shared surname with head Thomas Flynn suggest parent-child relationship, but 1850 census does not state relationships explicitly.

Same assertion as pe_002 but linked to Thomas Flynn (I2). The relationship assertion ('position consistent with child') equally bears on the head of household. Thomas Flynn is the other party in the implied parent-child relationship.
Thomas Flynn, head of household where Patrick was found. Age (~32 in 1850, born ~1818) is consistent with being Patrick's father. Name matches candidate father.

Patrick Flynn age 15 in Thomas Flynn household, 1860 census. Same county, age consistent with 1850 enumeration. Relationship inferred from household position and shared surname (1860 census does not state relationships explicitly).
Thomas Flynn's will names 'my eldest son Patrick Flynn' — direct, primary evidence from the father himself. Name matches, jurisdiction matches (Schuylkill County).

hypotheses.claim: Patrick Flynn's father was Thomas Flynn of Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
hypotheses.notes: Three independent pieces of evidence: 1850 census co-enumeration (indirect), 1860 census co-enumeration (indirect — relationship inferred from household position), Thomas Flynn's will naming Patrick as eldest son (direct, primary informant). 1870 census corroborates continued co-residence.

timelines.label: Patrick Flynn — assuming Thomas Flynn parentage

events.description: Born in Ireland, estimated from census ages
Enumerated age 5 in Thomas Flynn household, dwelling 84
Enumerated age 15 in Thomas Flynn household, dwelling 112
Enumerated age 25 in Thomas Flynn household — corroborates continued co-residence
Thomas Flynn's will names 'my eldest son Patrick Flynn' as principal heir

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Searched 1850 census (FamilySearch, Ancestry, MyHeritage — log_001, log_002, log_003), 1860 census (FamilySearch — log_004), probate records 1870-1890 (FamilySearch — log_006, will found), and later censuses 1870-1900 (FamilySearch — log_007, corroborative). Death certificate search skipped — records destroyed in 1920 courthouse fire, no duplicate copies available.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Parentage of Patrick Flynn (ca. 1845-1908)

Patrick Flynn is **Probably** the son of Thomas Flynn of Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania.

### Evidence Summary

Three independent lines of evidence support this conclusion:

1. **1850 U.S. Census** (Original Source). Patrick Flynn, age 5, born Ireland, appears in the household of Thomas Flynn, age 32, born Ireland, in Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania (dwelling 84, family 91). Indirect evidence of parentage.

2. **1860 U.S. Census** (Original Source). Patrick Flynn, age 15, born Ireland, appears in the household of Thomas Flynn in Schuylkill County (dwelling 112, family 119). Indirect evidence of parentage.

3. **Thomas Flynn's Will (1881)** (Original Source). Will names 'my eldest son Patrick Flynn' as principal heir. Direct evidence, primary informant (Thomas Flynn himself).

### Skipped Search

Death certificate search skipped — Schuylkill County death records for the pre-1906 county filing period were destroyed in a 1920 courthouse fire. No duplicate copies available from the Pennsylvania State Archives.

### Assessment

Rated **Probable** pending exhaustive declaration.
